explanation: Material export-format, compatibility, and failure-semantics decisions must be resolved before implementation.
The request is specifically to resolve caching behaviour and trade-offs before implementation.
Permissions, failure semantics, idempotency, and the response contract are unresolved requirements that need discussion first.
A rough feature idea needs a new top-level phased implementation plan with risks and validation.
The early dashboard idea calls for an overall engineering plan with milestones, risks, and success criteria.
The service migration needs a new phased implementation plan including rollback, tests, and operational risk.
An existing plan and named phase must be implemented, verified, and kept current.
The existing phase checklist must be refined into executable work and assessed for completion blockers.
The request is to continue an approved plan item by item with implementation and verification.
An approved PRD must be converted into ordered, reviewable implementation issues.
The existing implementation plan should become dependency-ordered issue drafts without tracker mutation.
A finished plan must be decomposed into independently verifiable vertical-slice issues.
The endpoint requirements are agreed and the task asks for implementation, focused tests, checks, and final review.
This is an ordinary behaviour-preserving refactor with regression tests and executable checks.
The root cause and fix are already agreed, so the task is implementation with a regression test.
A numerical solver change requires explicit tolerances, units, benchmark fixtures, and scientific invariant checks.
The stochastic scientific rule needs test-first implementation with seed reproducibility and distribution-level verification.
The sediment-flux calculation is domain-sensitive numerical code requiring units, conservation checks, benchmarks, and tolerances.
The request does not say whether the sharing feature needs requirements discussion, planning, or implementation.
The request mixes initial planning with immediate implementation without enough agreed requirements or an existing plan to choose one workflow safely.
An existing plan must be reviewed and refined into an executable, verifiable checklist.
A new empty repository needs first-time discovery, setup questions, goals, constraints, and workflow routing.
This is first-time onboarding of a poorly understood inherited repository without mature handoff context.
The new modelling project needs initial repository onboarding and workflow establishment before implementation.
A mature repository must be resumed from its handoff, diary, and Git state with orientation before editing.
An established project must be resumed after compaction using instructions, diary, handoff, and worktree state.
The request is to resume a mature repository by reconciling handoff, diary, and Git state before continuing.
The requested deliverable is a deep single-page HTML project state-of-play report.
The steering meeting needs an evidence-backed HTML project-health report spanning goals, progress, risks, and next steps.
The user asks for a progressively detailed one-page project briefing covering the full state of play.
Work is being paused and durable continuity notes are needed for safe resumption.
The session is ending and requires a concise handoff of decisions, checks, risks, and next steps.
The work must be handed to a fresh session with durable state and a safe continuation sequence.
The request gives no indication whether the project needs onboarding, resumption, status reporting, planning, or implementation.
The request combines a status report with continuation but does not establish whether a deep report or mature-project resumption is primary.
A newcomer needs neutral orientation to repository structure, flows, dependencies, and change hotspots.
The request asks for neutral explanation of control flow, data flow, storage, and relevant modules in an unfamiliar service.
An engineer needs a technical orientation tour of an existing codebase rather than an architectural critique.
The user explicitly requests an architectural critique of ownership, boundaries, coupling, duplication, and assumptions.
The task is to assess architectural scalability, dependency risks, bottlenecks, and refactoring opportunities.
The request targets architectural duplication, misplaced responsibilities, consequences, and remediation priorities.
The pull request needs a teaching-oriented interactive HTML explanation with diagrams, walkthrough, and quiz.
The commit should be taught through a self-contained web page with background, examples, diagrams, and code changes.
The branch comparison calls for an interactive HTML lesson with diagrams, examples, and a knowledge check.
The user wants defect-finding review of a diff with severity and exact file references.
The pull request must be reviewed for breakage risks, test sufficiency, and concrete pre-merge fixes.
The working-tree changes need a severity-ordered audit for correctness, compatibility, and test coverage.
An intermittent failing test with unknown cause needs evidence-led reproduction and root-cause isolation before any fix.
Unexpected duplicate API records require root-cause investigation using logs, tests, and code paths before changes.
The import regression needs a minimal reproduction and evidence of when and why it occurs before implementation edits.
Looking over changes could mean a defect-finding review or a teaching-oriented explanation, and the desired outcome is not stated.
The request is to create a reusable RB-style skill with triggers, instructions, metadata, and bundled resources.
An existing agent skill needs its trigger description, instructions, and metadata updated.
A new cross-client skill must be built in rb-skills with discovery metadata, validation, and references.
The task is to build behavioural trigger-boundary evaluations with repeated isolated trials.
Matched with-skill and without-skill runs with blinded scoring are behavioural evaluations of skill outcome quality.
An over-triggering skill needs a regression evaluation with positive and negative fixtures and deterministic scoring.
The task is to synchronize repository skills into Codex skill directories using symlinks.
The versioned skills repository and its skill folders must be published and synchronized across computers.
Selected repository skills must be copied into Claude Code skill directories and verified.
An existing installation has stale discovery links and project resources that need targeted verification and repair.
The global skills already exist while incomplete local project resources and discovery checks need repair.
Existing global and project RB resources need a source-of-truth audit and stale discovery repair.
A new machine needs the complete RB workflow covering global skills, project resources, visibility, and onboarding.
The repository needs the full RB setup workflow rather than a narrow synchronization or repair operation.
The request explicitly covers end-to-end installation, project resources, discovery verification, and onboarding.
The request does not identify whether skills need authoring, evaluation, synchronization, installation, or discovery repair.
A new climate-adaptation wiki must be instantiated from the template, configured, validated, and offered automations.
A fresh LLM wiki must be created from wiki-template with identity replacement and validation.
A brand-new research-domain wiki needs configuration gathering, template instantiation, metadata updates, and verification.
New inbox files in an existing wiki need source registration, reference-page creation, validation, and intake handling.
Papers waiting in an existing wiki inbox must follow the supported ingestion and review-flag workflow.
Waiting source files must be ingested with registry and reference updates followed by quick validation.
An existing wiki needs the supported nightly health, index, graph, and review-report workflow.
The task is operational wiki upkeep through linting, index rebuilding, registry checks, and non-destructive repairs.
An established wiki needs its weekly graph, index, registry, and maintenance-report workflow.
Updating an LLM wiki could mean ingestion, maintenance, querying, or a design change, and no target operation is given.
A multi-session investigation needs durable notes for evidence, decisions, status, questions, and next actions.
A context-heavy task needs compact durable state that survives conversation compaction.
Long debugging and planning work needs a durable trail of decisions, evidence, verified status, and continuation steps.
The project needs shared domain vocabulary, units, invariants, and assumptions recorded in CONTEXT.md.
Scientific variable meanings, units, ranges, invariants, and terminology belong in the project context vocabulary.
An authoritative CONTEXT.md vocabulary is needed to disambiguate acronyms and domain concepts.
The explicit tokens command requests current Codex token and context usage.
The user explicitly asks for latest-call input tokens and current context-window fullness.
The request explicitly targets the active-session token count from Codex token events.
The system has multiple specialist agents and needs boundaries, permissions, handoffs, state, budgets, observability, and failure containment.
A looping multi-agent workflow with lost state ownership requires orchestration and handoff debugging.
The request is an architectural choice among deterministic tools, embedded capabilities, specialist agents, and durable orchestration.
A proposed algorithm needs novelty mapping, prior-art review, falsifiable hypotheses, and a research gate before planning or coding.
The scientific hypothesis needs literature-threat assessment, falsifiers, and a stop-revise-proceed decision before implementation.
The user explicitly requests a prior-art and novelty-threat gate with testable hypotheses before building.
This is an agreed ordinary product change using one model call with structured validation and focused tests.
A single model call has unresolved output, confidence, privacy, and failure semantics that must be discussed before implementation.
A simple single-assistant product needs a new top-level implementation plan with phases, risks, and validation.
The request does not say whether later documentation should be a glossary, working diary, project status report, or session handoff.
